$comment: RTAM brand asset tree as data — the single source of truth. generate.py emits live-text source SVGs; outline.py emits *-outlined.svg distribution masters. Geometry is modelled ONCE per asset (runs + shapes, literal coords) with a list of colour-variant outputs. Bindu/positions are baked literals; derivation rules: Latin bindu cx = leading-R ink center, cy = baseline + fs*0.233, r = fs/12; Foundation wordmark descriptor x = RTAM advance-end + F6 gap 56; Devanagari forms carry NO bindu (ऋ is the self-contained vocalic-R).

inter.$comment: Inter-300 is not vendored; weight 300 maps to inter-400.ttf for outlining (outlined master approximates the donation caption at 400). Live text keeps weight 300 (renders Inter-300 where the web font is available).

outputs.title: Ṛ monogram in circle — gold ring on ivory
outputs.desc: Enclosed icon variant: charcoal R + gold bindu inside a gold ring. Used as a profile avatar, social media mark, and seal where a contained form reads better than a floating glyph.

outputs.title: Ṛ monogram in circle — charcoal ring on ivory
outputs.desc: Enclosed mono variant: charcoal R, charcoal bindu, charcoal ring. Single-color seal for stamps, embossing, and contexts that disallow gold.

outputs.title: Devanagari ऋ in circle — charcoal glyph, gold ring
outputs.desc: Enclosed seal variant: charcoal ऋ inside a gold ring. The Devanagari counterpart to the Latin circle-gold seal. Use as a sacred avatar, social profile mark, or stamped seal.

outputs.title: Ṛ monogram — gold
outputs.desc: Single-glyph icon mark in antique gold #C8A15A (R and bindu both gold). Use on ivory/sandstone for a warm, ceremonial mark.

outputs.title: Ṛ monogram — charcoal
outputs.desc: Single-glyph icon mark in charcoal #1A1A1A. Use on ivory/sandstone surfaces and for mono print.

outputs.title: Ṛ monogram — ivory
outputs.desc: Single-glyph icon mark in ivory #F7F3E9 on a transparent ground — a true overlay for charcoal / indigo / photographic dark surfaces. Place on a dark surface to see it; it is intentionally invisible on white.

outputs.title: Devanagari ऋ monogram — gold
outputs.desc: Sanskrit ऋ (vocalic R) — the Devanagari counterpart to the Latin Ṛ monogram. Represents the same sacred sound the bindu signifies under R; therefore no separate dot is added. Tiro Devanagari Sanskrit. All gold #C8A15A.

outputs.title: Devanagari ऋ monogram — charcoal
outputs.desc: Sanskrit ऋ in charcoal #1A1A1A. Use on ivory/sandstone surfaces and for mono print.

outputs.title: Devanagari ऋ monogram — ivory
outputs.desc: Sanskrit ऋ in ivory #F7F3E9 on a transparent ground — a true overlay for charcoal / indigo / photographic dark surfaces. Place on a dark surface to see it; intentionally invisible on white.

outputs.title: ṚTAM Foundation favicon
outputs.desc: Favicon: simplified Ṛ monogram at native 32x32. Sized so the R + bindu remain legible at 16px. Use a charcoal R on the page tab background; for hi-contrast tabs/manifests, swap the dark color via CSS.

assets.$comment: F6 microtypography APPLIED (US-15): descriptor x = RTAM advance-end (471.3, fs120 ls12) + locked gap 56 = 527 (was 540). Same gap brand-wide — the 3 Foundation-bearing lockups use x=527 too. Eye-checked; wordmark inline preview copies synced in the same commit.
assets.label: ṚTAM Foundation

outputs.title: ṚTAM Foundation — sacred wordmark (charcoal letters, gold bindu)
outputs.desc: Primary wordmark. The under-dot beneath R is the brand signature (bindu), drawn as an SVG circle so it renders independently of font availability. Clear-space rule: minimum padding equal to the cap-height of R on every side.

outputs.title: ṚTAM Foundation — pure-black wordmark (single-color print, fax, stamp)
outputs.desc: Single-color charcoal variant for mono print, fax, embossing, or stamp. Letters and bindu both #1A1A1A so the mark survives where color separation is unavailable.

outputs.title: ṚTAM Foundation — gold wordmark (all antique-gold)
outputs.desc: All-gold variant for premium / ceremonial contexts on ivory or dark backgrounds. Letters and bindu both #C8A15A.

outputs.title: ṚTAM Foundation — ivory wordmark for dark backgrounds
outputs.desc: Ivory variant on a transparent ground — a true overlay for charcoal / indigo / photographic backgrounds. Letters and bindu both #F7F3E9. Intentionally invisible on white; always preview on the intended dark surface.

outputs.label: RTAM Foundation
outputs.title: RTAM Foundation — public ASCII wordmark (no bindu)
outputs.desc: Public / English-only variant. No bindu — used in plain ASCII contexts (URLs, email signatures, legal documents) where the sacred mark is inappropriate or unreliable. Matches sacred variant geometry exactly so swaps remain visually consistent.

outputs.title: RTAM Foundation — Devanagari wordmark (Sanskritic register)
outputs.desc: Devanagari Foundation wordmark in the pure Sanskritic register: ऋतम् (name) + प्रतिष्ठान (pratiṣṭhāna, established institution). Default Devanagari register for sacred, scholarly, trust-deed contexts. No bindu — ऋ is self-contained.

outputs.title: RTAM Foundation — Devanagari wordmark (common register)
outputs.desc: Devanagari Foundation wordmark in the common Hindi register: ऋतम् (name) + फाउंडेशन (the borrowed transliteration of Foundation). For everyday, public-facing Hindi contexts. No bindu — ऋ is self-contained.

outputs.title: Rtambhareshvara Temple — Latin wordmark
outputs.desc: Stacked Latin wordmark for the temple: the name Rtambhareshvara (Cinzel, leading R carrying the gold bindu = the vocalic-ṛ crest) over the descriptor Temple. Charcoal text, gold bindu on transparent ground.

outputs.title: Ṛtambhareśvara Mandir — diacritic wordmark (pronunciation form)
outputs.desc: Mixed-diacritic pronunciation form: the leading R + gold bindu reads as Ṛ (vocalic-ṛ), the native Cinzel ś carries the palatal sibilant. Descriptor Mandir. The scholarly/sacred Latin register.

outputs.title: Rtambhareshvara Mandir — Devanagari wordmark
outputs.desc: Stacked Devanagari wordmark for the temple: ऋतम्भरेश्वर over मंदिर (Tiro Devanagari Sanskrit). No bindu — ऋ is the self-contained vocalic-R. Charcoal on transparent.

outputs.title: Bilingual lockup — ṚTAM Foundation / ऋतम् प्रतिष्ठान (pure Sanskritic register)
outputs.desc: Stacked bilingual lockup using the pure Sanskritic Devanagari form प्रतिष्ठान (pratiṣṭhāna — "established institution"), rather than the borrowed फाउंडेशन. For scholarly, sacred, or trust-deed contexts where the higher register is preferred.

outputs.title: Bilingual lockup — ṚTAM Foundation / ऋतम् फाउंडेशन
outputs.desc: Stacked bilingual lockup: Latin wordmark (sacred form with bindu) over a thin gold rule, with the common-register Devanagari name beneath. For use anywhere both audiences are addressed at once (website footer, certificate, letterhead).

assets.$comment: US-15: bindu added under the leading R (cx 64, measured leading-R ink center; cy = baseline + fs*0.233, r = fs/12 — same grammar as the temple wordmark); content shifted up 27u to vertically centre the lockup (was V-margins 108/54, now ~81/81). Devanagari axis verified centered (Δ0.5).
assets.label: Rtambhareshvara Mandir · ऋतम्भरेश्वर मंदिर

outputs.title: Temple lockup — Rtambhareshvara Mandir / ऋतम्भरेश्वर मंदिर
outputs.desc: Lockup for the temple name in both scripts. Wider viewBox (1280 vs the 1080 of the wordmark/bilingual lockups) to accommodate the longest name in the system. The leading R carries the gold bindu (the vocalic-ṛ crest); ASCII Latin form for legibility on physical signage and donor materials; full Devanagari beneath. Gold rule centered on the lockup axis.

outputs.title: Donation lockup — ṚTAM Foundation with supporting line
outputs.desc: Lockup for donation receipts, fundraising pages, and contribution forms. Sacred wordmark, thin gold rule, and a single quiet line of supporting copy. No CTA chrome — the lockup is a header, not a button.
